title: Unexpected memory shoot up after using match function
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
Usually works fine but in this instance, when used the following regular expression: `'^Gold\ Futures(\s+)\(1000\ troy\ oz\)$'` with match, it caused memory to shoot up.
The query was fired on a. distributed table but the query never reached other nodes. When tried to kill via client or console, that also failed.
The memory kept increasing till clickhouse crashed. Settings like `max_memory_usage` also failed to stop the query.
---
Link to fiddle: https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/ae7279e1-c9ae-4a74-9d8e-d43db09096cd
Here, with version 25.1.2.3 - I see some times the output returned is "The string did not match the expected pattern." but I also saw a couple of times that the query threw an exception (Tried to read after EOF).
When testing locally, this would cause OOM and crash the server as explained above.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
No
### How to reproduce
Use clickhouse version - 25.1.2.3
---
CREATE TABLE users (uid Int16, name String, age Int16, dob DateTime) ENGINE=ReplacingMergeTree
PRIMARY KEY (name, age);
INSERT INTO users VALUES (1231, 'John', 27, '1998-01-01');
INSERT INTO users VALUES (6666, 'Ksenia', 28, '1997-01-01');
INSERT INTO users VALUES (8888, 'Alice', 29, '1996-01-01');
INSERT INTO users VALUES (4444, 'Gold Futures   (1000 troy oz)', 10, '2015-01-01');
SELECT * FROM users WHERE match(name, '^Gold\ Futures(\s+)\(1000\ troy\ oz\)$');
---
### Expected behavior
Should give the output row for the corresponding user in this case.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE products (pid INT, description VARCHAR(255), price FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO products VALUES (1, 'Gold Futures   (1000 troy oz)', 1800.50, '2023-01-01 10:00:00');
INSERT INTO products VALUES (2, 'Silver Futures   (5000 troy oz)', 25.75, '2023-01-02 11:00:00');
SELECT * FROM products WHERE description REGEXP '^Gold Futures\s+\(1000 troy oz\)$';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM products WHERE price > 1000 AND created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '1' DAY;
key_bug_features: Using match function with specific regular expression
